original_hypothesis.title: Incomplete Order Struct Deletion in _cancelOrder Leaves Stale Data
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: _cancelOrder deletes only maker, baseToken, quoteToken, price, and amount fields but leaves remaining, prev, and next with stale values. Conversely, _removeFilledOrder clears maker, prev, and next but leaves baseToken, quoteToken, price, amount, and remaining. This inconsistency means cancelled/filled orders retain partial data in storage. While maker==address(0) prevents direct re-use in state-changing functions, the stale data could confuse off-chain indexers, view function consumers, or future contract upgrades.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Two cleanup functions for the same struct delete different fields, leaving inconsistent residual data. The proper pattern is delete orders[orderId] to zero all fields.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _cancelOrder deletes maker/baseToken/quoteToken/price/amount but not remaining/prev/next; _removeFilledOrder clears maker/prev/next but not others

preconditions: An order must be cancelled or fully filled
External system must read stale order data

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Partial struct field deletion instead of full delete orders[orderId]
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Stale remaining/prev/next in cancelled orders; stale baseToken/quoteToken/price/amount/remaining in filled orders
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Should use delete orders[orderId] for complete cleanup

analysis_questions: Can stale prev/next pointers cause linked list corruption?
Does any code path read remaining from a deleted order?
Could a future function be affected by residual data?
validation_requirements: Cancel an order, verify remaining/prev/next retain stale values
Fill an order completely, verify baseToken/quoteToken/price retain stale values
Confirm no active code path reads from deleted orders beyond maker check

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: After cancellation, getOrder(orderId) returns partial data (remaining non-zero, prev/next pointing to live orders) which could mislead off-chain systems into believing the order is partially active.
prerequisites: Off-chain system reading stale order data

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: No direct on-chain financial impact with current code
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Code fix to use delete orders[orderId]

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for complete struct cleanup after cancel/fill

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
